title: 10 best herbal creams for chills

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as muscle soreness and joint pain.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, cause blood vessels to dilate, which helps increase blood flow to the skin, helping to warm the body up and reduce chills.

Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial agents have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections, which may contribute to chills and fever; by combating these microorganisms, herbal ginger creams help promote a healthy recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with chills because of its natural warming and anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which stimulate the body's circulation and help to increase blood flow to the skin, thereby reducing the sensation of cold. Additionally, its analgesic and antispasmodic properties can help to soothe and relax the muscles, further alleviating the discomfort associated with chills. Regular application of ginger creams may provide relief and comfort, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to combat chills and fever by reducing the body's temperature and alleviating discomfort.

Curcuminoids (specifically Gingerol-6 and Shogaol-6): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate chills by protecting the body from oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part for its anti-inflammatory and warming properties, which help to relieve chills and body aches.
Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by chills.
Roots: Utilized for their medicinal properties, which aid in reducing fever and inflammation associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of chopped zingiber officinale roots in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of honey in a separate bowl.
Gradually add the infused liquid mixture to the beeswax-coconut oil mixture while stirring constantly until smooth.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat viral infections that can cause chills by reducing the replication of viruses and alleviating symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral, reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects, attributed to its flavonoids and terpenes, help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate chills.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with chills because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the body. The active compounds present in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and melissic acid, have natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to reduce body temperature and alleviate cold symptoms. By applying a Melissa officinalis cream to the skin, these compounds are absorbed, providing rapid relief from chills and promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate chills by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing fever and promoting a sense of calmness.

Linalool: A terpene found in Melissa officinalis, linalool exhibits antispasmodic and antiviral properties, which help to soothe the body and reduce chills associated with viral infections or other conditions.

Limonene: A monoterpene present in Melissa officinalis, limonene possesses antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat the underlying causes of chills and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, which provide antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe chills.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have a calming effect, helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Stems: Rich in essential oils and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in creams for chills.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of melissa officinalis flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil melt and emulsify.
Pour the infused melissa officinalis liquid into the cooled cream mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Cytokine Modulation: Lemongrass cream's cytokine-modulating property helps reduce inflammation and fever, alleviating chill symptoms by regulating the body's response to infection or injury.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of lemongrass cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which contributes to chills, pain, and discomfort during illness.

Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lemongrass cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help relieve body aches, joint pain, and stiffness associated with chills, making it an effective natural remedy for symptom relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with chills because of its natural properties. The antiviral and antibacterial properties in lemongrass help to combat the underlying cause of chills, which is often a viral or bacterial infection. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce body temperature and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass has a warming effect, which helps to stimulate blood circulation and relieve chills. This natural remedy provides a soothing and comforting relief from the symptoms of chills.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Citral is a terpene that acts as an antipyretic, helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to inflammation and fever.
Geraniol: Geraniol is a terpene that has a cooling effect, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate chills by dilating blood vessels and increasing blood flow to the skin.

Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that has a stimulating effect on the immune system, helping to increase the production of white blood cells and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the development of chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high citral content, which provides a cooling and refreshing effect.
Rhyzomes: Used due to their strong aroma and medicinal properties, which help to reduce chills and fever.
Stems: Used due to their ability to provide a steady release of essential oils, helping to maintain a consistent cooling effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and wash them thoroughly with distilled water for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the leaves for 2 hours to obtain 50g of essential oil.
In a heat-resistant container, melt 500g of cetyl alcohol and 250g of glycerin over low heat.
Mix 10g of essential oil with 100g of beeswax and heat the mixture for 10 minutes.
Combine the cooled mixture with the melted cetyl alcohol and glycerin mixture and stir until creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever and chills, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated skin.

Antimicrobial: Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infections and exacerbate chill symptoms, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.
Soothing and cooling: Aloe vera's gel has a calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce heat and discomfort caused by chills, and providing relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with chills because of its unique properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the skin. When applied topically, it can help to reduce body temperature by improving blood circulation and promoting sweat evaporation. Additionally, aloe vera creams can also provide hydration to the skin, which is often lost during chills, helping to alleviate the discomfort and promote a sense of well-being. This natural remedy is a popular alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce body temperature and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Terpenoids (specifically, aloin and aloe-emodin): These bioactive compounds have antipyretic properties, which can help lower body temperature and reduce chills by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in the regulation of body temperature.

Anthraquinones (specifically, aloe-emodin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help reduce chills and body temperature by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in gel that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps to relieve chills and skin irritation.
Gel (from Leaves): The clear, jelly-like substance inside the leaves has a cooling effect and helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with chills.
Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): A concentrated form of the gel that is often used in creams to provide instant relief from chills and soothe skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from 3-4 fresh aloe leaves and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Whip 1/2 cup of heavy cream until stiff peaks form and set aside for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted coconut oil mixture with the whipped cream and mix until smooth consistency is achieved.
Add the aloe vera gel and shea butter mixture to the combined mixture and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate chills by reducing body temperature and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial compounds, such as saponins and terpenes, help combat infections that can cause chills, promoting a healthy immune response and reducing fever.

Soften and moisturize skin properties: Pot marigold creams' emollient properties soothe and soften the skin, helping to reduce dryness and irritation that can exacerbate chill symptoms like shivering and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with chills because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with chills. Additionally, its ability to promote wound healing and support the skin's natural barrier function can help alleviate the discomfort caused by chills. This makes Calendula officinalis cream a popular natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin during episodes of chills.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can indirectly alleviate the discomfort and chill associated with colds and infections.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): As a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory, quercetin can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by blocking the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, calendulin): Calendulin, a sesquiterpene glycoside, has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to chills, thereby reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for chills due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for chills due to their ability to promote blood flow and reduce heat in the body, helping to alleviate chills.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for chills due to their content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Infuse the calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 1 cup 1 cup calendula flowers.
Strain the oil mixture after 2 to 3 weeks of infusion to remove the plant material.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as triterpenoids, which can help reduce body temperature and alleviate chill symptoms by decreasing inflammation in the body.

Antiviral activity: Kansas coneflower has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that may be causing chills, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
Immune system support: The cream contains bioactive compounds that can help stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and reduce the occurrence of chill symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with chills because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The antiviral properties in Echinacea angustifolia help to combat viral infections that cause chills. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing the discomfort associated with chills. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea angustifolia creams can help to alleviate chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and fever, which are often associated with chills, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Alkaloids (Cichorine): Alkaloids found in Echinacea angustifolia, such as cichorine, have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may cause chills.

Phenolic Acids (Caffeic Acid, Ferulic Acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of chills and fever by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in creams for chills due to their content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used in addition to roots and leaves due to their minor but still significant content of phenolic compounds, which contribute to the overall therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 500ml of boiling water to create an herbal infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth to remove solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Add 50ml of the echinacea angustifolia infusion to the melted wax and oil mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by chills.
Antibacterial and antiviral activity: The presence of antimicrobial compounds in dandelion cream helps to combat bacterial and viral infections that may be contributing to the onset of chills.

Warming and soothing properties: Dandelion's warming properties help to increase blood flow, reducing feelings of coldness and numbness associated with chills, while its soothing effects calm irritated skin and relieve discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with chills because it contains active compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and taraxasterol, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. These compounds help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as body aches and fatigue. Additionally, the cream's soothing and cooling effect on the skin can help to calm the body and promote a sense of relaxation, making it easier to recover from illness. It may also aid in promoting a healthy immune response.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce body temperature and alleviate chills associated with fever or infection.
Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help lower body temperature and relieve chills and fever.
Taraxasterol acetate: Taraxasterol acetate, another triterpenoid saponin, may exhibit antipyretic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to reduce chills and fever.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain inulin, a type of prebiotic fiber that can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make a tea or infusion that can help to reduce fever and alleviate chills due to their diaphoretic and antipyretic properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale root and 10 grams of dried flowers in a glass jar.
Mix the extracted plant material with 100 ml of jojoba oil and 50 ml of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before applying to the skin.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams can reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate chill symptoms such as fever, swelling, and pain, by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in chamomile cream help combat infections that may be causing chills, such as the common cold or flu, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.

Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile creams can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to chills, and improve overall sleep quality, helping the body to recover from illness more efficiently.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with chills because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredients in chamomile creams, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to calm the body's nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation. This can help to alleviate chills and fever by reducing the body's stress response and promoting a sense of calm and well-being. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help to relieve muscle cramps and spasms associated with chills.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce muscle tension and alleviate chills.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with chills.
Farnesene: A sesquiterpene that has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relax muscles and reduce fever, which may contribute to chills.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of apigenin, an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with chills.
Seeds: They are used for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the body and reduce inflammation caused by chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted supplier at 8 am.
Combine 100 grams of the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids immediately.
Blend the resulting infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter then heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues, relieving cold-induced discomforts such as aching muscles and joints.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic effects calm muscle cramps, spasms, and contractions caused by chills, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and coughs associated with chills, and making breathing easier.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with chills because of its warming and soothing properties. The active compounds in fennulic acid and anethole work together to increase blood circulation, which helps to counteract the cold and numb sensations associated with chills. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce muscle tension and discomfort, promoting overall relaxation and warmth. This natural remedy provides relief from chills, allowing the body to recover and feel more comfortable.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Bisabolol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate chills by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Anethole: Anethole acts as a vasodilator, helping to increase blood flow and reduce the sensation of chills by widening blood vessels and promoting sweating.
Fenchone: Fenchone has analgesic and antipyretic properties that help alleviate pain and reduce fever associated with chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relieve chills and body aches.
Leaves: They are often used in creams for their ability to reduce fever and alleviate chills due to the presence of essential oils and other compounds that have a cooling effect on the body.
Roots: They are sometimes used in creams for their potential to reduce fever, ease body aches, and promote sweating, which helps to alleviate chills and other symptoms associated with colds and flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse the foeniculum vulgare mixture in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused oil using a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix the filtered oil with 10% beeswax and 5% shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture using a hand mixer to achieve a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Allspice cream's antipyretic properties help to reduce fever and alleviate chills by counteracting the underlying inflammatory response.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in allspice cream, such as Eugenol and Cinnamic acid, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness.

Warmth-inducing properties: Allspice contains compounds that have a warming effect on the body, such as Cinnamaldehyde, which helps to increase blood flow and warmth, making it feel more comfortable when experiencing chills.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pimenta dioica, also known as allspice, creams helps with chills because of its unique combination of warming and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, particularly eugenol and acuminatin, work together to increase blood circulation, reduce muscle tension, and calm the nervous system. This helps to combat the cold, shivering sensation associated with chills, providing a soothing and comforting relief from the discomfort. Regular application can also help to boost overall immune function and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes, particularly eugenol, help with chills by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing fever and relieving discomfort associated with chills.

Phenolics: Phenolic compounds, including eugenol and isoeugenol, help with chills by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which may help combat underlying infections that can cause chills, as well as by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids, specifically eugenol, may help with chills by acting as a natural decongestant, helping to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure, which can contribute to the development of chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Allspice contain compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Fruits: The berries of Allspice contain oils and compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat chills and other cold-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20g of dried pimenta dioica leaves with 100ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks or until the liquid turns brown.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with chills because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the skin. The aroma of lavender has a profound effect on the body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to warm and comfort the skin, easing feelings of shivering and chilliness. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of lavender help to prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing, making it an effective treatment for cold-related skin issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lavender's antispasmodic property helps to relieve muscle tension and spasms caused by chills, providing relaxation and relief from discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever or flu-like symptoms, making it an effective remedy for soothing aching muscles and joints.
Anxiolytic property: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by chills, promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to create a calming and soothing effect in medicinal creams, helping to reduce chill symptoms.
Buds: Lavender buds contain a high concentration of essential oils that provide a cooling and relaxing sensation when applied topically, making them effective for relieving chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine one cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with two cups of water and bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and let it cool completely.
Add one cup of distilled water and two tablespoons of beeswax to the cooled liquid and gently warm the mixture over low heat stirring until the wax is fully melted.
Remove from heat and let it cool and thicken for about an hour before transferring to an airtight container for storage and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with chills because of its natural ability to warm and soothe the skin. The cream's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat infection and reduce inflammation, while its emollient qualities provide a calming and comforting sensation. As a result, damask rose creams can effectively alleviate chills by promoting warmth and relaxation, helping to restore balance to the body's temperature regulation system and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose creams have anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and ease cold-related discomforts, such as aches and pains.

Antimicrobial property: The creams contain antimicrobial agents like phenolic acids and terpenes, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms that can cause chills and fevers, promoting a healthy body response to illness.

Warming and soothing property: Damask rose creams contain natural emollients like fatty acids and waxes, which help warm and soothe the skin, providing relief from cold-induced numbness, tingling, or discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing fever and soothing the skin.
Buds: The buds are rich in flavonoids, which have natural antiseptic properties that help combat infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried rosa damascena petals and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Combine 100 grams of dried rosa damascena petals with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil at a temperature of 60 to 70 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids, then add 2 teaspoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture, then fill into jars and store in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with chills because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that work together to provide relief from shivering and body aches. The cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles, while its anti-inflammatory agents reduce swelling and ease pain. Additionally, the cream's warming sensation soothes the skin and promotes blood flow, helping to restore warmth and comfort to the affected areas. This natural remedy provides effective relief from chills without harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.
remedy_properties: Antiviral Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream contains eucalyptol, which has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against several viruses, helping to combat chill-inducing pathogens and reduce symptoms.

Decongestant Properties: The menthol content in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream helps to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure associated with chills, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Eucalyptus oil in the cream has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with chill-induced respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or pneumonia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort caused by chills.
Barks: Containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, barks provide antimicrobial properties that aid in treating infections contributing to chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the eucalyptus powder with 100ml of distilled water in a glass bowl, allowing it to steep for 15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing gently to extract the maximum amount of herbal essence and discarding the solids after 10 minutes.

Combine the filtered eucalyptus extract with 200g of unsalted butter and 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating it under low heat for 30 minutes until the mixture is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with chills because of its unique ability to soothe and warm the skin. The cream's active ingredient, boswellic acid, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, frankincense's natural warming effect can increase blood flow to the affected area, helping to dissipate cold temperatures and promote a sense of relaxation. As a result, using herbal frankincense creams can provide fast and effective relief from chills, helping to restore warmth and comfort to the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with chills.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of frankincense cream can help combat infections that may be contributing to chills, such as the common cold or flu.

Warming and analgesic: Frankincense oil has a warming effect when applied topically, which can help relieve muscle and joint pain associated with chills, while also providing a soothing and comforting sensation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them effective in relieving chill-related discomfort.

Barks: The barks of the frankincense plant have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and chills, as they possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to soothe the skin and calm the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin extract from mature trees during peak resin production season.
Mix 200 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Add 20 grams of boswellia serrata powder to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature over 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency.
Transfer the final cream to sterilized jars and seal with tight-fitting lids for storage and dispensing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with chills because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and tannins, help to soothe the skin and reduce swelling, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, wild geranium has a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce fever and promote a sense of warmth and comfort. As a result, herbal wild geranium creams provide fast and effective relief from chills, making them a popular natural remedy for individuals suffering from colds, flu, or other illnesses that cause shivering and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild geranium cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by chills, allowing for a faster recovery.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties in the cream help to relax muscles and relieve cramping, which can be common symptoms of chills.
Vasoconstrictor properties: Wild geranium's vasoconstrictor properties help to narrow blood vessels and improve circulation, which can aid in warming up the body and relieving chilliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing rich antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe body aches and reduce fever associated with chills.
Rhyzomes: Acting as a natural antipyretic, rhyzomes help regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chilliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried geranium maculatum flowers at 50 grams for each 100 milliliters of cream base.
Combine the dried flowers with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for a 24-hour infusion time.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and combine it with 80 grams of beeswax for melting at 170 degrees fahrenheit.
Whip the mixture until smooth, adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for a calming effect and 100 milliliters of distilled water to achieve the desired consistency.
Fill clean glass jars with the geranium maculatum cream and store them in an airtight container for up to six months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with chills because of its unique ability to soothe and warm the skin. The natural ingredients found in elder, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, work together to calm the body's natural response to cold temperatures, reducing feelings of shivering and discomfort. Additionally, elder creams can help to increase blood circulation, which can further aid in warming the body from the inside out, providing relief from chills and promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder cream's antiviral properties help to combat viral infections such as colds and flu, which can cause chills by reducing the replication of viruses and inhibiting their ability to attach to host cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of elder cream reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with chills such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of infections and exacerbate chills by reducing inflammation and promoting overall immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the skin, making them effective in relieving chills.

Barks: The bark of the elder plant contains tannins and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for creating creams that combat chill-induced infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves then dry them properly for two weeks.
Combine dried flowers and leaves with jojoba oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Steep mixture for 30 minutes under low heat before straining it through cheesecloth.
Mix strained infusion with vitamin e oil and coconut oil then whip until smooth.
Pour whipped cream into sterilized jars sealing them tightly within five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenes, work to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by chills. Additionally, stinging nettle creams contain antihistamines that help to relieve itching and redness associated with chills. By providing instant relief from discomfort, stinging nettle creams offer a natural and effective way to manage chills, making them an excellent remedy for individuals seeking a gentle and non-pharmaceutical solution.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms like chills and fever.
Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: The cream's antiviral and antimicrobial properties can help combat viral infections that cause chills, reducing their severity and duration.

Vasoconstrictive effects: Stinging nettle cream's vasoconstrictive properties can help reduce blood flow to the skin, which may alleviate symptoms like chilliness, shakiness, and excessive sweating associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for reducing chills and fever.
Seeds: Stinging nettle seeds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as joint pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1/4 cup of olive oil in a clean glass jar and store it at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the strained liquid and heat it in a double boiler until the wax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to stimulate blood flow and relax muscles. The herbs in the cream, such as Origanum majorana, contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the skin and can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with colds and flu. Additionally, the aromatic oils present in wild marjoram can be invigorating and uplifting, helping to promote feelings of warmth and comfort when applied topically.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wild marjoram's antispasmodic compounds help to relax muscles, relieve spasms, and ease cramps, which can help alleviate chill-induced muscle tension.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of wild marjoram cream can help thin and loosen mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, and providing relief from chills and congestion.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram's antiseptic and antimicrobial compounds help to protect against infections, reducing the risk of secondary illnesses that can arise from being chilled.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming the body during chills.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce fever and alleviate chill symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh origanum vulgare leaves and stems, cleaning them thoroughly with water.
Combine the herbs with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through cheesecloth.
Mix the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into sterile tubes or jars, allowing to solidify completely at room temperature within 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with chills because of its natural warming and soothing properties. The herb thyme contains compounds such as thymol, which has been traditionally used to relieve cold-induced chills. When applied topically in a cream form, thyme's warmth and calming effects help to counteract the sensations of chilliness, providing rapid relief from shivering and discomfort. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it also helps to promote blood flow and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its effectiveness in combatting chills.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains compounds like carvacrol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of chills such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Antiviral properties: Thymol has also been found to have antiviral activity against certain viruses, including those that may be causing chills in the first place, helping to shorten the duration and severity of the illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in essential oils that help to stimulate blood circulation and warm the body, making them effective in relieving chills.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and fever associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested thyme leaves with 120 milliliters of olive oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Melt 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler while stirring occasionally over low heat until fully liquefied after 10 minutes.
Combine the infused thyme oil with the melted beeswax and stir until well combined then pour into sterilized jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for chills are topical remedies infused with various plant extracts that help alleviate chills by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being. The best herbal creams for chills are formulated with a blend of natural ingredients such as Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Capsicum annuum, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Achillea millefolium, and Rosmarinus officinalis. If left untreated, chills can lead to fatigue, decreased productivity, and a weakened immune system, negatively impacting daily life, work, and social activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal creams are arthritis, muscle soreness, and inflammation. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort can help alleviate symptoms like joint pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with these conditions. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may provide relief from discomfort and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with chills is a blend of Peppermint and Eucalyptus oil infused creams. Peppermint oil helps to reduce fever and ease body aches, while Eucalyptus oil provides a cooling sensation and helps to open up airways. Adding a small amount of Ginger and Cinnamon oil infused cream can also aid in warming the body and promoting circulation, helping to alleviate chills and soothe the body. This combination provides a balanced approach to healing chills naturally.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with chills because they harness the potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of ginger. When applied topically, these creams can help to warm the skin and reduce muscle tension, relieving the discomfort associated with chills. The capsaicin in ginger also stimulates circulation, allowing the body to regulate its temperature more effectively. By providing a soothing and warming sensation, Zingiber officinale creams can offer relief from chills, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps reduce chills by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, which eases body aches and reduces fever.
Shogaol: Contributes to reducing chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate body pain and discomfort associated with chills.
Volatile oil (specifically, zingiberene and bisabolol): Helps alleviate chills by exhibiting warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help increase blood circulation and reduce muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make creams for chills because they contain high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and warming properties.
Roots: Roots are used to make creams for chills because they contain gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce pain and promote blood circulation.
Stems: Stems are used to make creams for chills because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes and 200ml of distilled water for the base ingredient.
Melt 50g of beeswax in a double boiler at 180-190°f for 10 minutes to create a stable base.
Combine the melted beeswax with 200ml of distilled water and 100g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes in a mixing bowl.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes to ensure all ingredients are well combined and evenly distributed.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains alkylamides and polysaccharides that stimulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of chills. It also helps to lower body temperature by promoting blood circulation and reducing inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have antipyretic properties, which help to alleviate fever and chills associated with colds, flu, and other viral infections. This makes it a popular natural remedy for cold and flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help with chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and interleukins, thereby reducing the body's inflammatory response to infections.
Alkylamides: These compounds help with chills by modulating the body's immune response, reducing the severity of symptoms associated with infections, such as fever and chills.

Flavonoids: These compounds help with chills by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with infections, and promoting overall immune system function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are primarily used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are used for their ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold and flu.
Leaves: Echinacea purpurea leaves are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe and calm the body during cold and flu episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from the plant in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until crispy.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make tea.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Combine the cooled echinacea tea with the melted beeswax mixture and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to create the herbal cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with chills because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol and eucalyptol present in Eucalyptus globulus creams create a cooling sensation on the skin, which can help reduce fever and alleviate chills. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help soothe and calm the body, reducing muscle aches and pains associated with chills. This can provide relief and comfort, making it easier to manage chills and recover from underlying illnesses.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with chills by acting as a decongestant and reducing inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate the symptoms of chills.
Cineole: It helps with chills by acting as an antipyretic and antiviral agent, which can help reduce fever and relieve the body from viral and bacterial infections that may cause chills.

Catechin: It helps with chills by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress in the body, both of which can contribute to chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used to make creams for chills due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides a decongestant and anti-inflammatory effect.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used to make creams for chills due to their high tannin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and fever.
Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are also used to make creams for chills due to their expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil of eucalyptus globulus from dried leaves by steam distillation for 2-3 hours.
Mix 20 grams of the extracted essential oil with 80 grams of a base oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture to thicken the cream and improve its texture.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Pour the hot mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and set before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with chills because of its warming properties, which are derived from the capsaicin content. When applied topically, capsaicin stimulates the nerve endings, causing a release of heat and increasing blood flow to the affected area. This process helps to counteract the cold and relax the muscles, providing relief from chills and associated discomfort. The cream's ability to generate heat also helps to improve circulation, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps with chills by causing vasodilation, which increases blood flow to the skin and helps to warm the body.
Capsaicinoid: It helps with chills by acting as a neurotransmitter inhibitor, blocking the transmission of pain signals and reducing the sensation of cold.
Quercetin: It helps with chills by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and promoting the body's natural warming response.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits (chilli peppers) are commonly used due to their high capsaicin content, which provides a warming sensation and helps relieve chills.
Seeds: Seeds contain a higher concentration of capsaicin compared to the fruits and are often used to make creams due to their potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used in some herbal remedies to relieve chills, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are ripe and have reached full color.
Clean and chop 250g of fresh capsicum annuum fruits into small pieces using a knife.
Combine 250g of chopped capsicum annuum with 200ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 2 hours at 50-60°c to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth and store the resulting herbal cream in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with chills because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which work together to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. This increased blood circulation helps to warm the body's extremities, relieving chills and promoting a sense of overall well-being. As a natural remedy, Ginkgo biloba creams can provide quick relief from chills without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate chills by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and enhancing the body's natural response to cold stress.
Terpenoids: By acting as antioxidants, terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba creams help protect the body from oxidative damage caused by cold temperatures, contributing to a reduction in chills.
Bilobalide: This constituent helps to improve blood flow to extremities, which can help alleviate the sensation of chills by promoting warmth and reducing cold-related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are most commonly used due to their high flavonoid content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help alleviate chills and fever.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used for their ginkgotoxin content, which is thought to have antiviral properties that can help combat viral infections that may cause chills.
Buds: Ginkgo buds are used due to their flavonoid content, which is believed to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to facilitate herbal extraction process.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solid particles.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a stable emulsifier base.
Blend the herbal oil with the emulsifier base and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with chills because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which work together to soothe and calm the skin. The licorice root extract in these creams has a natural warming effect, which can help to counteract the cold and clammy feeling associated with chills. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help to lock in moisture and reduce discomfort, providing quick relief from chills and promoting overall skin health. This makes them an effective remedy for cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce inflammation and fever, which are often associated with chills, by modulating the immune response and increasing blood circulation.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate chills by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Glycyrrhizin: It has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help combat chills by regulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, making it easier for the body to recover from fever and other symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a key ingredient that helps reduce inflammation and fever, making them suitable for creams to treat chills.
Leaves: Some herbal products use leaves to create a soothing and calming effect, which can help alleviate the symptoms of chills and fever.
Barks: Barks from the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant are sometimes used to create a relaxing and anti-inflammatory cream, which can be beneficial in treating chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse 250ml of distilled water with 25 grams of the cleaned glycyrrhiza glabra roots for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully melted and combined.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until a smooth consistent cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with chills because they contain quinine, a natural compound extracted from the bark of the Cinchona tree. Quinine has been traditionally used to treat fever and chills by increasing the body's metabolic rate and enhancing the immune system's response to illness. The cream's antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce body temperature, alleviate chills, and soothe the skin. This natural remedy provides a comforting and non-invasive way to manage chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinoline alkaloids: These compounds help reduce fever and alleviate chills by acting as antipyretics, which are substances that reduce body temperature.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate chills and reduce the overall discomfort associated with fever and inflammation.
Tannins: Tannins have a drying effect on the body, which can help reduce the feeling of chilliness and promote a sense of warmth by constricting blood vessels near the surface of the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain quinine, a key compound used to treat chills and fever.
Barks: The bark of Cinchona officinalis contains quinine and other alkaloids that help to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinchona officinalis are also a source of quinine and other compounds that help to treat chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh cinchona leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 cup of dried cinchona leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1/4 cup of shea butter with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of cinchona powder with 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler on low heat for 5 minutes.
Stir the mixture for 2 minutes and pour it into small containers to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with chills because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antiviral and antibacterial agent that can help alleviate symptoms associated with chills. The oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may be causing the chill, while its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the body and reduce fever. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to hydrate and calm the skin, providing relief from the discomfort and aches often associated with chills. This can lead to a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): A terpene that helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins.
Cymene: A terpene that exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve pain associated with chills.
Linalool: An alcohol that has a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills by promoting the release of heat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil extracted from the leaves is used due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat chills.
Buds: Buds are used for their high concentration of cineole, a compound with decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate chills.
Barks: The barks of the Melaleuca alternifolia tree are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in treating chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Melt 10% beeswax in 30g of coconut oil at 150-170°f for 10-15 minutes in a double boiler.
Combine 40g of distilled water and 2% melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a separate heat-proof bowl.
Gradually add the water and essential oil mixture to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Continue to heat the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until it reaches 120-140°f and thickens slightly.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool to room temperature completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams helps with chills because they contain the anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of the herb, also known as yarrow. The soothing and warming effects of the cream help to reduce body temperature and alleviate the discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, the cream's antiviral and antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the chills, promoting overall health and well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various health issues.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate chills.
Phenolic acids (e.g., chlorogenic acid, caffeic acid): These antioxidants help regulate body temperature, reduce inflammation, and combat the underlying causes of chills.
Terpenoids (e.g., bornyl acetate, limonene): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate chills and reduce body temperature.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and warm the skin.
Flowers: Utilized for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in reducing muscle tension and relieving chills.
Stems: Employed for their warming and stimulating properties, which help increase blood circulation and relieve coldness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in early morning to capture highest essential oil content.
Steep 1 cup of dried achillea millefolium in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids to obtain 1.5 cups of herbal oil.
Combine 1 cup of herbal oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of emulsifier in a double boiler.
Whisk the mixture until it reaches 120-140°f then pour into a mold to set and cool for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and warming properties. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, work together to stimulate blood circulation and increase body heat. This helps to relieve cold and clammy feelings, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. Additionally, rosemary's antispasmodic properties can also help to relax muscles and soothe the body, further reducing feelings of chilliness and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the body's response to chills and alleviate associated discomfort.
Rosmarinic acid: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to combat the underlying causes of chills and promote a sense of warmth.
Bornyl acetate: It has a warming effect on the body, which can help to counteract the feeling of coldness associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which provide antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and warm the body.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain essential oils and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and provide a warming sensation to combat chills.

Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and warm the skin, reducing the symptoms of chills and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried rosmarinus officinalis in 250ml of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the infused oil to the beeswax and shea butter mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and set completely.
